verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 encodes beta-1,4-galactosyltransferase 1, a Golgi-resident type II single-pass TM glycosyltransferase responsible for lactosylceramide and N-glycan galactosylation. Its canonical localization is the trans-Golgi network. However, B4GALT1 has a well-documented second existence on the cell surface: it is constitutively present on the plasma membrane of many cell types (including transformed and normal cells), where it functions as a surface galactosyltransferase that binds polylactosamine on sperm (during fertilization), growth factor receptors, and other substrates. This surface pool is documented by cell-surface biotinylation, flow cytometry on intact cells, and functional studies. The TM domain anchors the protein, and in the PM it is oriented with its catalytic domain facing outward. This qualifies as dual_localization: dominant Golgi pool plus a real, documented PM surface pool. Several surface-function studies and drug/probe programs target this surface form.
